LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 22, 2014 TO GIVE DEFENDANTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C. DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 11, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F).JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBIT 3 IN SUPPORT OF COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARISE FROM DEFENDANTS ENGAGING IN LITIGATION BY FILING ANTI-SLAPP MOTIONS AND MOTIONS FOR STATUTORY ATTORNEYS' FEES. (COMPLAINT, P. 1.) ENGAGING IN LITIGATION IS A PROTECTED ACTIVITY. (CCP SEC. 425.16(E)(1).) PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING A POSSIBILITY OF PREVAILING. THE COMPLAINT SEEKS TO HAVE THE TRIAL COURT VACATE FINAL DECISIONS OF THE COURT OF APPEAL. PLAINTIFFS CITE NO AUTHORITY THAT WOULD ALLOW FOR SUCH RELIEF. DEFENDANTS ARE AWARDED REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $13,374.00 PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 425.16(C). PLAINTIFFS ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

COSTS OF PREPARATION ON APPEAL DEPOSITED (TRANSACTION ID # 56667588) BY RESPONDENT U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ("U.S. BANK") U.S. BANCORP U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION (INCORECTLY SUED HEREIN AS U.S. BANK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED. BASED ON THE SUPPORTING EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH THE MOTION, THE COURT AWARDS ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $28,000. CCP SECTION 425.16(C)'S ATTORNEY'S FEES PROVISION INCLUDES ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED BY THE PREVAILING DEFENDANT IN AN APPEAL OF AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. (SEE WILKERSON V. SULLIVAN (2002) 99 CAL.APP.4TH 443, 448.) DEFENDANTS ARE THE PREVAILING PARTY BECAUSE THE COURT OF APPEAL DISMISSED PLAINTIFFS' APPEAL IN DEFENDANTS' FAVOR WITHOUT MODIFICATION. (GUTIERREZ DECLARATION EX. U.) IN ITS OPPOSITION PLAINTIFFS DO NOT ADDRESS THE SUBSTANCE OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION. INSTEAD, PLAINTIFFS CONTEND THAT THE COURT SHOULD DEFER TO THE COURT OF APPEAL'S DECISION TO INTERVENE IN THE MATTER, WITHOUT CITING EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT ITS POSITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. 302/EHG

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Petitioner George McKray telephone number 510-769-8461 appeared in Propria Persona.
Jill Gutierrez of Dorsey & Whitney LLP telephone number 714-800-1400 appeared by telephone for Defendants U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION.

Mr. McKray makes a statement on the record to support his position. Upon conclusion, the Court confirmed its tenative ruling. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S motion for attorney's fees for defending the appeal of the November 2, 2015 order awarding fees and costs to the defendants is granted in part. Defendants are awarded a total of $4,563.55 consisting of fees of $3500 and costs of $1,063.55. The fees of $3500 are calculated at 10 hours of combined attorney and paralegal time at a reasonable, if not high, blended hourly rate for an associate attorney and paralegal of $350 per hour. While there is little doubt that defendants were the prevailing parties on the appeal and thus are entitled to an award of fees and costs for the services reasonably required by their attorneys to defend the appeal, there is likewise little doubt that all defendants' counsel had to do to effectively serve defendants' interests was to file a bare-bones motion to dismiss. The history of this and the prior related cases demonstrated, with little need for defendants to do anything other than bring the procedural history of the cases up to date, that the court of appeal would likely grant a motion to dismiss the appeal. Nor was it necessary for defendants to do much by way of motion papers to succeed on this motion for fees. Defendants are best advised in any future litigation with plaintiffs not to "take the bait" and have their counsel do a full defense or offense for matters that don't require it. All of plaintiffs' arguments opposing the motion, including the request to continue this motion, lack merit. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Motion For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - GEORGE A. McKRAY, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JILL A. GUTIERREZ OF THE DORSEY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-714-800-1422 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT AND U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Attorney's Fees is granted. Based on the supporting evidence submitted with the motion, the court awards attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $28,000. CCP section 425.16(c)'s attorney's fees provision includes attorney's fees incurred by the prevailing defendant in an appeal of an anti-SLAPP motion. (See Wilkerson v. Sullivan (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 443, 448.) Defendants are the prevailing party because the Court of Appeal dismissed Plaintiffs' appeal in Defendants' favor without modification. (Gutierrez Declaration Ex. U.) In its opposition Plaintiffs do not address the substance of Defendants' motion. Instead, Plaintiffs contend that the court should defer to the Court of Appeal's decision to intervene in the matter, without citing evidence to support its position. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Continued To Sep-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Have Pltf George A Mcray Declared A Vexatious Litigant
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael R. Tenerelli, Esq. of Dorsey & Whitney LLP, Tel. 714.800.1400, for defendants and moving party;
George Alexander Mckray, Esq. of the Law Office of George Mckray, Tel. 510.769.8461, for plaintiff and also appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT, U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Have Plaintiff George A Mckray Declared A Vexatious Litigant is denied without prejudice. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants seek to declare Plaintiff McKray a vexatious litigant pursuant to CCP sec. 391(b)(2). Although he has filed multiple lawsuits on behalf of Plaintiffs Anthony Lamperti and Elizabeth Lamperti, this is the second lawsuit where he is a pro per plaintiff. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael R. Tenerelli, Esq. of Dorsey & Whitney LLP, Tel. 714.800.1400, for defendants and moving party;
George Alexander Mckray, Esq. of the Law Office of George Mckray, Tel. 510.769.8461, for plaintiff and also appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling in its entirety. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT and U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted. Defendants' unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiffs' Request for Judicial Notice of Exhibit 3 in Support of Complaint is granted. The allegations in the Complaint arise from Defendants engaging in litigation by filing anti-SLAPP motions and motions for statutory attorneys' fees. (Complaint, p. 1.) Engaging in litigation is a protected activity. (CCP sec. 425.16(e)(1).) Plaintiffs have not met their burden of showing a possibility of prevailing. The Complaint seeks to have the trial court vacate final decisions of the Court of Appeal. Plaintiffs cite no authority that would allow for such relief. Defendants are awarded reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $13,374.00 pursuant to CCP sec. 425.16(c). Plaintiffs are jointly and severally liable. (pREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2014 Continued To Apr-22-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT and U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is continued to April 22, 2014 to give Defendants the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6C.
